docs.title: Welcome to Krewlyzer
docs.text:
Krewlyzer is a robust, user-friendly command-line toolkit for extracting a wide range of biological features from cell-free DNA (cfDNA) sequencing data. It is designed for cancer genomics, liquid biopsy research, and clinical bioinformatics, providing high-performance, reproducible feature extraction from BAM files.

Krewlyzer draws inspiration from cfDNAFE and implements state-of-the-art methods for fragmentation, motif, and methylation analysis, all in a modern Pythonic interface with rich parallelization and logging.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Motif Analysis: End motifs, breakpoint motifs, and diversity scores.
  • Fragment Size Analysis: Coverage (FSC), Ratios (FSR), and Distributions (FSD).
  • Nucleosome Positioning: Windowed Protection Scores (WPS).
  • Tissue of Origin: Orientation-aware Fragmentation (OCF).
  • Methylation: Fragment-level methylation patterns (UXM).
  • Mutant Analysis: Mutant vs. Wild-type fragment size comparison (mFSD).

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text:
• Linux or macOS (tested on Ubuntu 20.04, macOS 12+)
  • Python 3.8+
  • ≥16GB RAM recommended for large BAM files
  • Docker (optional, for easiest setup)

docs.title: With Docker (Recommended)
docs.text:
docker pull ghcr.io/msk-access/krewlyzer:latest
# Example usage:
docker run --rm -v $PWD:/data ghcr.io/msk-access/krewlyzer:latest motif /data/sample.bam -g /data/hg19.fa -o /data/motif_out

docs.title: With uv / pip
docs.text:
uv venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install krewlyzer
Or install from source: 
git clone https://github.com/msk-access/krewlyzer.git
cd krewlyzer
uv pip install .

docs.title: Citation & Acknowledgements
docs.text: If you use Krewlyzer in your work, please cite this repository and cfDNAFE.

docs.title: Primary Literature
docs.text:
Krewlyzer implements or adapts methods from the following papers:

  1. DELFI (FSR): Mouliere F, Chandrananda D, Piskorz AM, et al. Enhanced detection of circulating tumor DNA by fragment size analysis. Sci Transl Med. 2018;10(466):eaat4921. DOI
  2. WPS: Snyder MW, Kircher M, Hill AJ, Daza RM, Shendure J. Cell-free DNA Comprises an In Vivo Nucleosome Footprint that Informs Its Tissues-Of-Origin. Cell. 2016;164(1-2):57-68. DOI
  3. OCF: Sun K, Jiang P, Chan KC, et al. Orientation-aware plasma cell-free DNA fragmentation analysis in open chromatin regions informs tissue of origin. Genome Res. 2019;29(3):418-427. DOI
  4. UXM: Sun K, et al. Fragment-level methylation measures cell-of-origin and cancer-specific signals in cell-free DNA. Nature. 2023;616(7956):563-571. DOI

docs.title: Acknowledgements
docs.text: Krewlyzer was developed by the MSK-ACCESS team at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center.

docs.title: Run All Features
docs.text: The run-all command allows you to execute all feature extraction modules for a single BAM file in one go.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
krewlyzer run-all sample.bam --reference hg19.fa --output all_features_out \
    [--variant-input variants.vcf] \
    [--threads N] \
    [--type SE|PE]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
• sample.bam: Input BAM file (sorted, indexed).
  • --reference, -g: Reference genome FASTA.
  • --output, -o: Output directory.
  • --variant-input, -v: (Optional) VCF or MAF file for mfsd analysis.
  • --threads, -t: Number of threads (default: 1).
  • --type: Fragment type for UXM (SE or PE).

docs.title: Nextflow Pipeline
docs.text: Krewlyzer includes a Nextflow pipeline (krewlyzer.nf) for processing multiple samples in parallel on HPC clusters or local machines.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: nextflow run krewlyzer.nf --samplesheet samplesheet.csv --ref /path/to/reference.fa

docs.title: Samplesheet Format
docs.text:
A CSV file with the following columns: 

sample_id,bam,vcf
sample1,/path/to/sample1.bam,/path/to/sample1.vcf
sample2,/path/to/sample2.bam,
- sample_id: Unique identifier. - bam: Path to BAM file. - vcf (or maf): Optional path to variant file.

docs.title: Profiles
docs.text:
• -profile lsf: For LSF clusters.
  • -profile slurm: For SLURM clusters.
  • -profile docker: Run using Docker container.

docs.title: File Not Found Error
docs.text: Error: FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory Solution: Ensure all input files (BAM, FASTA, BED) exist and paths are correct. Use absolute paths to avoid ambiguity.

docs.title: Permission Error
docs.text: Error: PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied Solution: Check that you have write permissions for the output directory.

docs.title: Missing Dependencies
docs.text:
Error: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named '...' Solution: Ensure Krewlyzer is installed in your current environment. 

uv pip install krewlyzer
Or use the Docker image.

docs.title: Reference Mismatch
docs.text: Issue: Results look wrong or empty. Solution: Ensure your BAM files and the reference FASTA are from the same genome build (e.g., both hg19 or both hg38). Krewlyzer defaults to hg19 for provided data files.

docs.title: Memory Errors
docs.text: Issue: Process crashes on large BAM files. Solution: Increase available RAM (≥16GB recommended). Reduce the number of threads if running in parallel.

docs.title: Command Summary
docs.text: Command Description motif Motif-based feature extraction fsc Fragment size coverage fsr Fragment size ratio fsd Fragment size distribution wps Windowed protection score ocf Orientation-aware fragmentation uxm Fragment-level methylation (SE/PE) mfsd Mutant fragment size distribution run-all Run all features for a BAM

docs.title: Reference Data
docs.text:
• Reference Genome (FASTA):
  • Download GRCh37/hg19 from UCSC
  • BAMs must be sorted, indexed, and aligned to the same build
  • Bin/Region/Marker Files:
  • Provided in krewlyzer/data/ (see options for each feature)

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
# 1. Motif extraction (produces .bed.gz files)
krewlyzer motif sample.bam -g hg19.fa -o motif_out

# 2. Extract additional features from motif output:
krewlyzer fsc motif_out --output fsc_out
krewlyzer fsr motif_out --output fsr_out
krewlyzer fsd motif_out --arms-file krewlyzer/data/ChormosomeArms/hg19_arms.bed --output fsd_out
krewlyzer wps motif_out --output wps_out
krewlyzer ocf motif_out --output ocf_out
krewlyzer uxm /path/to/bam_folder --output uxm_out
krewlyzer mfsd sample.bam --input variants.vcf --output mfsd_out/sample.mfsd.tsv

# 3. Run all features in one call:
krewlyzer run-all sample.bam --reference hg19.fa --output all_features_out --variant-input variants.vcf

docs.title: Fragment Size Coverage (FSC)
docs.text: Command: krewlyzer fsc

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Computes z-scored coverage of cfDNA fragments in different size ranges, per genomic bin (default: 100kb), with GC correction.

docs.title: Biological Context
docs.text:
cfDNA fragment size profiles are informative for cancer detection and tissue-of-origin. FSC quantifies the coverage of: - Short: 65-150bp - Intermediate: 151-260bp - Long: 261-400bp - Total: 65-400bp

Values are normalized to genome-wide means.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: krewlyzer fsc motif_out --output fsc_out [options]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• --bin-input, -b: Bin file (default: data/ChormosomeBins/hg19_window_100kb.bed)
  • --windows, -w: Window size (default: 100000)
  • --continue-n, -c: Super-bin size (default: 50)
  • --threads, -t: Number of processes

docs.title: Fragment Size Distribution (FSD)
docs.text: Command: krewlyzer fsd

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Computes high-resolution (5bp bins) fragment length distributions per chromosome arm.

docs.title: Biological Context
docs.text: cfDNA fragmentation patterns at chromosome arms can reflect nucleosome positioning, chromatin accessibility, and cancer-specific fragmentation signatures.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: krewlyzer fsd motif_out --arms-file krewlyzer/data/ChormosomeArms/hg19_arms.bed --output fsd_out [options]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• --arms-file, -a: Chromosome arms BED (required)
  • --threads, -t: Number of processes

docs.title: Fragment Size Ratio (FSR)
docs.text: Command: krewlyzer fsr

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Calculates the ratio of fragment counts in specific size bins relative to the total count per genomic window.

docs.title: Biological Context
docs.text: The DELFI method (Mouliere et al., 2018) showed that cfDNA fragment size ratios are highly informative for cancer detection. Krewlyzer calculates ratios for: - Ultra-Short: 65-100bp (Highly specific for ctDNA) - Short: 65-150bp - Intermediate: 151-260bp - Long: 261-400bp

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: krewlyzer fsr motif_out --output fsr_out [options]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• --bin-input, -b: Bin file (default: data/ChormosomeBins/hg19_window_100kb.bed)
  • --windows, -w: Window size (default: 100000)
  • --continue-n, -c: Super-bin size (default: 50)
  • --threads, -t: Number of processes

docs.title: Mutant Fragment Size Distribution (mFSD)
docs.text: Command: krewlyzer mfsd

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Compares the size distribution of mutant vs. wild-type reads at variant sites.

docs.title: Biological Context
docs.text: Mutant ctDNA fragments are typically shorter than wild-type cfDNA. This module quantifies this difference using high-depth targeted sequencing data, providing a sensitive marker for ctDNA presence. It performs a Kolmogorov-Smirnov (KS) test to statistically compare the distributions.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: krewlyzer mfsd sample.bam --input variants.vcf --output output.tsv [options]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• --input, -i: VCF or MAF file (required).
  • --format, -f: Input format ('auto', 'vcf', 'maf'). Default: 'auto'.
  • --map-quality, -q: Minimum mapping quality (default: 20).

docs.title: Motif-based Feature Extraction
docs.text: Command: krewlyzer motif

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Extracts end motif, breakpoint motif, and Motif Diversity Score (MDS) from sequencing fragments.

docs.title: Biological Context
docs.text: Motif analysis of cfDNA fragment ends can reveal tissue-of-origin, nucleosome positioning, and mutational processes. MDS quantifies motif diversity, which may be altered in cancer.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
krewlyzer motif path/to/input.bam -g path/to/reference.fa -o path/to/output_dir \
    --minlen 65 --maxlen 400 -k 3 --verbose

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
• EDM/: End Motif frequencies
  • BPM/: Breakpoint Motif frequencies
  • MDS/: Motif Diversity Scores
  • *.bed.gz: Intermediate file used for other modules (FSC, FSR, FSD, WPS, OCF).

docs.title: Orientation-aware Fragmentation (OCF)
docs.text: Command: krewlyzer ocf

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Computes orientation-aware cfDNA fragmentation (OCF) values in tissue-specific open chromatin regions.

docs.title: Biological Context
docs.text: OCF (Sun et al., Genome Res 2019) measures the phasing of upstream (U) and downstream (D) fragment ends in open chromatin, informing tissue-of-origin of cfDNA.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: krewlyzer ocf motif_out --output ocf_out [options]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• --ocr-input, -r: Open chromatin region BED (default: data/OpenChromatinRegion/7specificTissue.all.OC.bed)
  • --threads, -t: Number of processes

docs.title: Fragment-level Methylation (UXM)
docs.text: Command: krewlyzer uxm

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Computes the proportions of Unmethylated (U), Mixed (X), and Methylated (M) fragments per region.

docs.title: Biological Context
docs.text: Fragment-level methylation (UXM, Sun et al., Nature 2023) reveals cell-of-origin and cancer-specific methylation patterns in cfDNA.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Single-end (default)
krewlyzer uxm /path/to/bam_folder --output uxm_out [options]

# Paired-end mode
krewlyzer uxm /path/to/bam_folder --output uxm_out --type PE [options]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• --mark-input, -m: Marker BED file (default: data/MethMark/Atlas.U25.l4.hg19.bed)
  • --map-quality, -q: Minimum mapping quality (default: 30)
  • --min-cpg, -c: Minimum CpG per fragment (default: 4)
  • --methy-threshold, -tM: Methylation threshold (default: 0.75)
  • --unmethy-threshold, -tU: Unmethylation threshold (default: 0.25)
  • --type: Fragment type: SE or PE (default: SE)
  • --threads, -t: Number of processes

docs.title: Windowed Protection Score (WPS)
docs.text: Command: krewlyzer wps

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Computes nucleosome protection scores (WPS) for each region in a transcript/region file.

docs.title: Biological Context
docs.text: The WPS (Snyder et al., 2016) quantifies nucleosome occupancy and chromatin accessibility by comparing fragments spanning a window to those ending within it. - High WPS: Nucleosome protection. - Low WPS: Open chromatin / nucleosome depletion.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: krewlyzer wps motif_out --output wps_out [options]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• --tsv-input: Transcript region file (default: data/TranscriptAnno/transcriptAnno-hg19-1kb.tsv)
  • --wpstype: WPS type (L for long [default], S for short)
  • --threads, -t: Number of processes
